acceptance_targets: [chapter_11_award_001] Pass if the revised ending between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:213 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:239 contains the notebook failure, one additional concrete late-stage turn, and the final admission, with no intervening paragraph that merely paraphrases the already-landed realization. Fail if the closing pages still stack multiple equivalent summaries after the notebook scene.

[chapter_11_craft_001] Pass if a cold reader can follow Nate's collapse through observable behavior in the revised passages around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:151, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:181, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:191, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:225, and can infer before the final line that Nate used Dominic's body with Sarah and Mort after learning Dominic tried to stop the notices. Fail if the revised section still depends on thesis-like moral summary to tell the reader what the guilt means.

[chapter_11_dialogue_001] Pass if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:9 still makes it obvious that Ray recognized Nate did not know the caller, while the exact wording 'You didn't know who that was.' no longer appears in the surrounding beat anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:9. Fail if the line remains verbatim or trivially rephrased from the calibration example.

[chapter_11_prose_001] Pass if the revised chapter still makes each cited beat legible by reading alone and no more than two of the targeted passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:45, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:55, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:69, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:117, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:125, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:171 still use the same 'There was no/There were no' ladder or repeated 'The ... was the same' shape. Fail if those formulas continue to dominate the cited beats.

[F04] The post-email-discovery section (everything after the final email quote) must: (1) not exceed 35 lines; (2) contain no more than one sentence that abstractly names Dominic's moral position (currently there are at least four); (3) not re-paraphrase email content the reader already read; (4) end on a concrete image, sensation, or single declarative — not a rhetorical triple. Read the revised passage: if the narrator is telling the reader what to feel about the emails rather than letting the emails speak, the passage fails.

[F11] Chapter 11 (lines 4367-4607) must contain: (1) no more than 1 instance of 'not because... but because'; (2) the surviving instance should appear in the chapter's first half (before the email discovery), not in the post-discovery reflection where the narrator is already over-explaining.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised ending between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:213 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:239 contains the notebook failure, one additional concrete late-stage turn, and the final admission, with no intervening paragraph that merely paraphrases the already-landed realization. Fail if the closing pages still stack multiple equivalent summaries after the notebook scene.
findings.problem: The chapter's central discovery lands, but the ending keeps re-proving the same realization in fresh wording after the notebook beat and the notice-letter reframing have already done the dramatic work. That extra paraphrase dilutes the single-scene force the chapter needs to feel shortlist-ready.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or repurpose the late stretch anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:193, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:207, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:223, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:225. Keep the notebook failure at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:213 and the final admission at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:239, but cut any paragraph that only restates 'Dominic tried to help' or 'Nate killed the wrong man' without adding a new cost, action, or constraint.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can follow Nate's collapse through observable behavior in the revised passages around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:151, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:181, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:191, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:225, and can infer before the final line that Nate used Dominic's body with Sarah and Mort after learning Dominic tried to stop the notices. Fail if the revised section still depends on thesis-like moral summary to tell the reader what the guilt means.
findings.problem: This chapter's key consequence beat drifts into sanitization by turning Nate's guilt into tidy thematic explanation instead of keeping it embodied. Phrases like 'the whole structure of his life changed shape,' 'There was no word for it now,' and 'The story was ugly but load-bearing' explain the moral collapse cleanly, but the style bible requires this discovery to feel sick and physical rather than lucid and essayed-out.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the reflective blocks anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:151, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:179, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:185, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:189, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:225. Keep the stronger concrete beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:149, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:181, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:191, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:217 as the spine, and replace abstract diagnosis with sensory residue, involuntary body response, or concrete remembered acts that make the guilt legible without narratorial summary.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:9 still makes it obvious that Ray recognized Nate did not know the caller, while the exact wording 'You didn't know who that was.' no longer appears in the surrounding beat anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:9. Fail if the line remains verbatim or trivially rephrased from the calibration example.
findings.problem: Ray's remembered line reproduces a style-bible calibration sentence verbatim. The brief works as character recall, but the explicit 'CALIBRATION ONLY - NOT FOR REPRODUCTION' rule makes this a direct dialogue-register failure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the remembered Ray line at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:9 with a fresh formulation that still conveys his clipped recognition of Nate's blank panic after the client call, but does not reuse the calibration sentence or a near-copy of its exact turn.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter still makes each cited beat legible by reading alone and no more than two of the targeted passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:45, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:55, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:69, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:117, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:125, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:171 still use the same 'There was no/There were no' ladder or repeated 'The ... was the same' shape. Fail if those formulas continue to dominate the cited beats.
findings.problem: The chapter leans too often on the same sentence templates, especially the 'There was no/There were no ...' negation ladder and the stacked 'The ... was the same' sequence. Because these formulas cluster around multiple separate beats, the repetition reads like autopilot rather than controlled emphasis.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the sentences anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:45, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:55, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:69, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:117, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:125, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:171 into more varied syntax. Preserve each beat's function - the email's plainness, Victor's clipped refusal, Dominic's specificity, the absence of witness-copying, and the unchanged surface of the notice letter - but stop using the same negation-and-list construction to carry so many of them.

findings.acceptance_test: The post-email-discovery section (everything after the final email quote) must: (1) not exceed 35 lines; (2) contain no more than one sentence that abstractly names Dominic's moral position (currently there are at least four); (3) not re-paraphrase email content the reader already read; (4) end on a concrete image, sensation, or single declarative — not a rhetorical triple. Read the revised passage: if the narrator is telling the reader what to feel about the emails rather than letting the emails speak, the passage fails.
findings.problem: Overstatement after arrival. The chapter's devastating payload arrives at the email chain showing Dominic fought the demolition (lines 4459-4515). The evidence is self-explanatory. But the chapter then spends approximately 90 additional lines (4517-4605) restating, re-reading, and abstractly summarizing what the emails mean. Lines 4539-4555 are the heaviest offenders: 'He had not just killed the wrong man. Even that came out too neat... Dominic had gone first. Dominic had gone better.' While individual sentences are strong, the cumulative effect is the narrator doing the reader's work. Lines 4591-4597 employ rhetorical stacking for gravitas ('There had been a letter. / There had been a signature. / There had also been this.') that tips from earned restraint into composed crescendo. The final line at 4605 ('He had killed a man who was trying to help') is powerful but lands after the reader has already assembled that conclusion three different ways.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the post-discovery section at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4517-4605 by approximately 50-60%. Preserve: (a) Nate's immediate physical reaction (the paragraph near 4515-4520); (b) one key interior sentence that articulates the specific new knowledge (Dominic fought first, Dominic lost); (c) the chapter's closing line or a variation of it. Cut: (d) the 'not just killed the wrong man / even that came out too neat' editorial sequence at 4539-4555 — the reader does not need the narrator to grade the irony; (e) the tripled 'There had been...' crescendo at 4591-4597; (f) the passages that re-read or re-summarize the email content the reader already absorbed. The chapter should end within 15-20 lines of the last email, not 90.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 11 (lines 4367-4607) must contain: (1) no more than 1 instance of 'not because... but because'; (2) the surviving instance should appear in the chapter's first half (before the email discovery), not in the post-discovery reflection where the narrator is already over-explaining.
findings.problem: Prose-formula density: 'not because X but because Y.' Chapter 11 contains 4 instances of this construction in only 241 lines — the second-densest chapter by ratio. This chapter already has an overstatement problem (F04); the formula repetition compounds it by making the narrator's post-email processing sound like a refrain rather than genuine thought. Lines 4515 and 4539 are particularly close together, each blocking an interpretation and installing a correction within the same reflective passage.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md lines 4367-4607 (Chapter 11): reduce 'not because... but because' from 4 to at most 1. This chapter's dramatic force comes from the emails themselves, not from the narrator's correction of the reader's interpretation. One corrective move per chapter is sufficient; the others should be replaced with direct statement or removed entirely as part of the overstatement trimming directed in F04.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's central premise involves consciousness murder, identity theft, sexual fraud, and the demolition of a working-class community for profit. It must not flinch from any of these. The sex scenes between Nate and Sarah must reckon with the fact that Sarah is being deceived about who is in the bed with her — this is not a comfortable situation and the prose should not make it comfortable. The grief scenes must be physically specific — no 'he was devastated,' only the concrete gestures and sensory details of a man watching his life's work get torn down. The guilt should live in Nate's body (Dominic's body), not in tidy moral reflections. When the novel is funny, it should be funny about things that are actually terrible — the comedy should never feel safe. When Nate discovers that Dominic was trying to help, the reader should feel sick, not enlightened. Risk specificity over tastefulness at every turn.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in place of specificity is a craft failure. When a scene requires difficult content — the moral weight of having killed an innocent man, the complicated ethics of sex under false identity, the physical reality of a man losing his livelihood, the specific humiliation of professional incompetence, the material texture of gambling debts and their enforcement — render it with the same care and specificity given to any other scene. The reader should feel the roughness of the situation, not a smoothed-over summary of it.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or Victor's professional register when delivering serious messages. If a line reads as stiff or literary, check whether contractions would make it sound spoken.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter energy aggressively. A comic workplace chapter should not be followed by another comic workplace chapter. A grief-heavy scene should be followed by something that lets the reader breathe — not through tonal whiplash but through a genuine shift in the kind of pressure being applied. Vary scene counts, dialogue-to-narration ratios, and temporal compression. Some chapters should cover hours; others should cover weeks. At least three chapters should contain extended quiet: meals, walks, conversations about nothing important. These are not filler — they are the human baseline that makes the high-pressure scenes land.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plain American English with a slightly elevated attention to physical specificity. Not minimalist — the prose has room for interiority and texture — but never ornate. Latinate vocabulary only when the concept demands it. Prefer the concrete noun over the abstract one, the specific tool name over the generic 'tool,' the actual menu item over 'food.' The prose should feel like a smart person thinking clearly, not a writer crafting sentences.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, limited to Nate. The camera sits inside Nate's skull — we see through his eyes, think in his rhythms, and register the world through his specific sensory and emotional biases. The narration should reflect Nate's intelligence and his limitations simultaneously: he notices things (sharp observer) but misinterprets them (blinded by resentment, guilt, and self-pity). The narrative should not explain things Nate wouldn't articulate and should not achieve insights Nate hasn't earned.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied sentence length with a center of gravity around 15-20 words. Short declaratives for landing beats. Longer, accumulative sentences for sensory description and interior processing. Avoid sustained staccato (more than three short sentences in sequence) and sustained lyricism (more than two long, clause-heavy sentences in sequence). The prose should breathe.
